useCases.title: Daily Task Triage
useCases.summary: Engineer reviews and prioritizes their task queue at the start of each day
useCases.description: Engineer reviews and prioritizes their task queue at the start of each day

scenario.context: Engineer starts their workday and needs to know what to focus on
scenario.before: Engineer checks Slack, email, Jira, and calendar to piece together priorities
scenario.after: Engineer opens TaskFlow, sees AI-sorted task list, and starts working within 2 minutes
scenario.impact: Saves 20 minutes daily per engineer on task triage

useCases.trigger: Engineer opens the TaskFlow Pro dashboard at the start of their workday
preconditions: Engineer has an active TaskFlow account
At least 5 tasks are assigned to the engineer

mainFlow.action: System displays AI-prioritized task list for the day
Engineer reviews top 3 tasks and adjusts if needed
Engineer clicks 'Start Focus' to begin work on the top task

alternativeFlows.name: Manual Priority Override
steps: Engineer disagrees with AI ranking and manually reorders tasks
System records the override as training data for the AI model

exceptionFlows.name: No Tasks Assigned
exceptionFlows.trigger: Engineer has zero tasks in their queue
exceptionFlows.handling: System shows a 'No tasks assigned' message with a link to the team backlog for self-assignment

businessRules: AI priority considers deadline proximity, dependency blocking count, and effort estimate
useCases.notes: This is the most common user journey — optimizing this flow is critical for adoption.
